sanskrit:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः।
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम्॥
transliteration: nāhaṃ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ | mūḍho 'yaṃ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam ||
literal_meaning: Not / I / manifest / to everyone / by yoga-māyā / covered / foolish / this / does not know / world / Me / unborn / inexhaustible
contextual_meaning: The deepest layer of reality (Kṛṣṇa as pure awareness) is always present but remains invisible to minds clouded by habitual patterns (yoga-māyā). Only when attention is purified does the unchanging, birthless ground of being become self-evident.
psychological_state: Cognitive humility: recognizing that the mind’s default settings filter out subtler dimensions of experience, and that deliberate inner work is required to perceive what is always already here.
trigger_scenario: A high-functioning professional meditates daily yet feels stuck; a chance encounter with a simple gardener whose palpable peace reveals the limitation of conceptual knowledge alone.
anchor_text: Pure awareness hides behind your thoughts; look past them to meet the changeless.
